author: codex-root

patch.value: pm-governance-audit comments-audit limit contract contradicts runtime alias semantics
Reconcile comments-audit pagination controls so agents can independently bound scanned items and emitted full-history rows, while preserving old spellings through an explicit compatibility policy.

Runtime, help, package contracts, completions, and docs define one truthful pagination grammar; --limit-items remains the scanned-item cap; a canonical row-output cap bounds full-history rows without lowering scan coverage; legacy --limit behavior remains available as a documented hidden/deprecated alias or a migration hint; conflicting legacy/canonical values return a targeted structured recovery; focused tests cover independent caps and compatibility
The documented scanned-item and output-row limits apply independently, allowing complete item coverage with a bounded row payload.
The command exits because --limit and --limit-items must match, revealing that runtime treats them as aliases despite help describing different scopes.
Large-workspace audits cannot independently control scan completeness and token/output size, and agents waste a retry discovering the hidden alias rule.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate check evidence: all-status searches for comments-audit limit semantics found closed implementation item pm-w1j3 and broad output-contract item pm-srns,but no active issue for the live package help/runtime contradiction. Reproduced during the full 6,546-comment-row audit.

message: Track live package audit pagination-contract defect
